source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, $\angle BAC = \angle ABC = 42^\circ$. Through point C, draw $CD \perp AB$ at point D. Let point E be a point on $CD$. If $\triangle ACE$ is folded along $AE$ to obtain $\triangle AFE$ and points E, F, B are exactly on the same line, connect $CF$. What is the measure of $\angle CFA$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} \\
Since $CD \perp AB$\\
$\therefore \angle ADC = 90^\circ$ \\
Since $\angle BAC = \angle ABC = 42^\circ$\\
$\therefore \angle ACD = 90^\circ - \angle BAC = 48^\circ$, $AC = BC$\\
Since $CD \perp AB$\\
$\therefore \angle BCD = \angle ACD = 48^\circ$ \\
In $\triangle ACE$ and $\triangle BCE$\\
$\left\{\begin{array}{l}
AC = BC\\
\angle ACD = \angle BCD\\
CE = CE
\end{array}\right.$ \\
$\therefore \triangle ACE \cong \triangle BCE$\\
$\therefore \angle CAE = \angle CBE$ \\
Since folding $\triangle ACE$ along $AE$ yields $\triangle AFE$,\\
$\therefore \angle AFE = \angle ACD = 48^\circ$, $\angle CAE = \angle FAE$, $AC=AF$\\
$\therefore \angle CAE = \angle FAE = \angle CBE$\\
Let $\angle CAE = \angle FAE = \angle CBE = x$\\
$\therefore \angle BAF = \angle BAC - \angle CAE - \angle FAE = 42^\circ - 2x$, $\angle ABF = \angle ABC - \angle CBE = 42^\circ - x$ \\
Since $\angle AFE = \angle BAF + \angle ABF = 42^\circ - 2x + (42^\circ - x) = 84^\circ - 3x$ \\
$\therefore 48^\circ = 84^\circ - 3x$\\
$\therefore x = 12^\circ$ \\
$\therefore \angle CAF = \angle CAE + \angle FAE = 24^\circ$ \\
Since $AC=AF$\\
$\therefore \angle CFA = \angle FCA = \frac{180^\circ - \angle CAF}{2} = \boxed{78^\circ}$

需要结合构造与折叠性质，利用三角形全等、对应角相等、反射后边角关系继续推理。
后续还要设未知角并联立角度关系求解，最后再利用等腰三角形求目标角，属于明显多步推导。

解答链条主要由标准几何关系组成，如垂直、全等、折叠对称、角和关系，过程可逐步检查。

normalization_record.normalized_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, $\angle BAC = \angle ABC = 42^\circ$. Through point C, draw $CD \perp AB$ at point D. Let point E be a point on $CD$. If $\triangle ACE$ is folded along $AE$ to obtain $\triangle AFE$ and points E, F, B are exactly on the same line, connect $CF$. What is the measure of $\angle CFA$?
normalization_record.normalized_answer_text: Since $CD \perp AB$, $\angle ADC = 90^\circ$. Since $\angle BAC = \angle ABC = 42^\circ$, $\angle ACD = 90^\circ - \angle BAC = 48^\circ$, and $AC = BC$. Since $CD \perp AB$, $\angle BCD = \angle ACD = 48^\circ$. In $\triangle ACE$ and $\triangle BCE$, we have $AC = BC$, $\angle ACD = \angle BCD$, and $CE = CE$, so $\triangle ACE \cong \triangle BCE$. Therefore $\angle CAE = \angle CBE$. Since folding $\triangle ACE$ along $AE$ yields $\triangle AFE$, $\angle AFE = \angle ACD = 48^\circ$, $\angle CAE = \angle FAE$, and $AC = AF$. Therefore $\angle CAE = \angle FAE = \angle CBE$. Let $\angle CAE = \angle FAE = \angle CBE = x$. Then $\angle BAF = \angle BAC - \angle CAE - \angle FAE = 42^\circ - 2x$, and $\angle ABF = \angle ABC - \angle CBE = 42^\circ - x$. Since $\angle AFE = \angle BAF + \angle ABF = 84^\circ - 3x$, we get $48^\circ = 84^\circ - 3x$, so $x = 12^\circ$. Thus $\angle CAF = \angle CAE + \angle FAE = 24^\circ$. Since $AC = AF$, $\angle CFA = \angle FCA = \frac{180^\circ - \angle CAF}{2} = 78^\circ$.

node_records.canonical_value: What is the measure of $\angle CFA$?
surface_forms: What is the measure of $\angle CFA$?

normalized_value.target_text: What is the measure of $\angle CFA$?
normalized_value.expected_answer: Since $CD \perp AB$, $\angle ADC = 90^\circ$. Since $\angle BAC = \angle ABC = 42^\circ$, $\angle ACD = 90^\circ - \angle BAC = 48^\circ$, and $AC = BC$. Since $CD \perp AB$, $\angle BCD = \angle ACD = 48^\circ$. In $\triangle ACE$ and $\triangle BCE$, we have $AC = BC$, $\angle ACD = \angle BCD$, and $CE = CE$, so $\triangle ACE \cong \triangle BCE$. Therefore $\angle CAE = \angle CBE$. Since folding $\triangle ACE$ along $AE$ yields $\triangle AFE$, $\angle AFE = \angle ACD = 48^\circ$, $\angle CAE = \angle FAE$, and $AC = AF$. Therefore $\angle CAE = \angle FAE = \angle CBE$. Let $\angle CAE = \angle FAE = \angle CBE = x$. Then $\angle BAF = \angle BAC - \angle CAE - \angle FAE = 42^\circ - 2x$, and $\angle ABF = \angle ABC - \angle CBE = 42^\circ - x$. Since $\angle AFE = \angle BAF + \angle ABF = 84^\circ - 3x$, we get $48^\circ = 84^\circ - 3x$, so $x = 12^\circ$. Thus $\angle CAF = \angle CAE + \angle FAE = 24^\circ$. Since $AC = AF$, $\angle CFA = \angle FCA = \frac{180^\circ - \angle CAF}{2} = 78^\circ$.

normalized_value.title: MM-Math 开放题
normalized_value.rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, $\angle BAC = \angle ABC = 42^\circ$. Through point C, draw $CD \perp AB$ at point D. Let point E be a point on $CD$. If $\triangle ACE$ is folded along $AE$ to obtain $\triangle AFE$ and points E, F, B are exactly on the same line, connect $CF$. What is the measure of $\angle CFA$?
normalized_value.expected_answer: Since $CD \perp AB$, $\angle ADC = 90^\circ$. Since $\angle BAC = \angle ABC = 42^\circ$, $\angle ACD = 90^\circ - \angle BAC = 48^\circ$, and $AC = BC$. Since $CD \perp AB$, $\angle BCD = \angle ACD = 48^\circ$. In $\triangle ACE$ and $\triangle BCE$, we have $AC = BC$, $\angle ACD = \angle BCD$, and $CE = CE$, so $\triangle ACE \cong \triangle BCE$. Therefore $\angle CAE = \angle CBE$. Since folding $\triangle ACE$ along $AE$ yields $\triangle AFE$, $\angle AFE = \angle ACD = 48^\circ$, $\angle CAE = \angle FAE$, and $AC = AF$. Therefore $\angle CAE = \angle FAE = \angle CBE$. Let $\angle CAE = \angle FAE = \angle CBE = x$. Then $\angle BAF = \angle BAC - \angle CAE - \angle FAE = 42^\circ - 2x$, and $\angle ABF = \angle ABC - \angle CBE = 42^\circ - x$. Since $\angle AFE = \angle BAF + \angle ABF = 84^\circ - 3x$, we get $48^\circ = 84^\circ - 3x$, so $x = 12^\circ$. Thus $\angle CAF = \angle CAE + \angle FAE = 24^\circ$. Since $AC = AF$, $\angle CFA = \angle FCA = \frac{180^\circ - \angle CAF}{2} = 78^\circ$.

field_audit_records.before_value: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, $\angle BAC = \angle ABC = 42^\circ$. Through point C, draw $CD \perp AB$ at point D. Let point E be a point on $CD$. If $\triangle ACE$ is folded along $AE$ to obtain $\triangle AFE$ and points E, F, B are exactly on the same line, connect $CF$. What is the measure of $\angle CFA$?
field_audit_records.after_value: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, $\angle BAC = \angle ABC = 42^\circ$. Through point C, draw $CD \perp AB$ at point D. Let point E be a point on $CD$. If $\triangle ACE$ is folded along $AE$ to obtain $\triangle AFE$ and points E, F, B are exactly on the same line, connect $CF$. What is the measure of $\angle CFA$?

field_audit_records.before_value:
\textbf{Solution:} \\
Since $CD \perp AB$\\
$\therefore \angle ADC = 90^\circ$ \\
Since $\angle BAC = \angle ABC = 42^\circ$\\
$\therefore \angle ACD = 90^\circ - \angle BAC = 48^\circ$, $AC = BC$\\
Since $CD \perp AB$\\
$\therefore \angle BCD = \angle ACD = 48^\circ$ \\
In $\triangle ACE$ and $\triangle BCE$\\
$\left\{\begin{array}{l}
AC = BC\\
\angle ACD = \angle BCD\\
CE = CE
\end{array}\right.$ \\
$\therefore \triangle ACE \cong \triangle BCE$\\
$\therefore \angle CAE = \angle CBE$ \\
Since folding $\triangle ACE$ along $AE$ yields $\triangle AFE$,\\
$\therefore \angle AFE = \angle ACD = 48^\circ$, $\angle CAE = \angle FAE$, $AC=AF$\\
$\therefore \angle CAE = \angle FAE = \angle CBE$\\
Let $\angle CAE = \angle FAE = \angle CBE = x$\\
$\therefore \angle BAF = \angle BAC - \angle CAE - \angle FAE = 42^\circ - 2x$, $\angle ABF = \angle ABC - \angle CBE = 42^\circ - x$ \\
Since $\angle AFE = \angle BAF + \angle ABF = 42^\circ - 2x + (42^\circ - x) = 84^\circ - 3x$ \\
$\therefore 48^\circ = 84^\circ - 3x$\\
$\therefore x = 12^\circ$ \\
$\therefore \angle CAF = \angle CAE + \angle FAE = 24^\circ$ \\
Since $AC=AF$\\
$\therefore \angle CFA = \angle FCA = \frac{180^\circ - \angle CAF}{2} = \boxed{78^\circ}$
field_audit_records.after_value: Since $CD \perp AB$, $\angle ADC = 90^\circ$. Since $\angle BAC = \angle ABC = 42^\circ$, $\angle ACD = 90^\circ - \angle BAC = 48^\circ$, and $AC = BC$. Since $CD \perp AB$, $\angle BCD = \angle ACD = 48^\circ$. In $\triangle ACE$ and $\triangle BCE$, we have $AC = BC$, $\angle ACD = \angle BCD$, and $CE = CE$, so $\triangle ACE \cong \triangle BCE$. Therefore $\angle CAE = \angle CBE$. Since folding $\triangle ACE$ along $AE$ yields $\triangle AFE$, $\angle AFE = \angle ACD = 48^\circ$, $\angle CAE = \angle FAE$, and $AC = AF$. Therefore $\angle CAE = \angle FAE = \angle CBE$. Let $\angle CAE = \angle FAE = \angle CBE = x$. Then $\angle BAF = \angle BAC - \angle CAE - \angle FAE = 42^\circ - 2x$, and $\angle ABF = \angle ABC - \angle CBE = 42^\circ - x$. Since $\angle AFE = \angle BAF + \angle ABF = 84^\circ - 3x$, we get $48^\circ = 84^\circ - 3x$, so $x = 12^\circ$. Thus $\angle CAF = \angle CAE + \angle FAE = 24^\circ$. Since $AC = AF$, $\angle CFA = \angle FCA = \frac{180^\circ - \angle CAF}{2} = 78^\circ$.
